DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AARON R. JACKSON OF THE JACKSON LAW GROUP, P.C., TEL. NO. 1-415-379-0560 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAMAY LEE OF THE BARULICH DUGONI LAW GROUP, INC., TEL. NO. 1-650-292-2900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STEVEN A. LAUB, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Steven Laub's demurrer to all causes of action in the first amended complaint is overruled in its entirety. Defendant Steven Laub argues that all of plaintiff Ling Li's claims are time-barred because CCP 366.2(a) requires commencement of an action against an estate's personal representative within one year of a decedent's death. Section 366.2(a), however, is "concerned only with the time within which an action on a liability of the decedent may be brought, not with the proper parties in such a case." 22 Cal. L.Rev. Comm. Reports 895 (1992). The reference to the decedent's "representatives" was deleted from former Section 353(b). (Id. ) Mr. Laub is attempting to engraft language onto section 366.2(a) that is not there. (Burgos v. Tamulonis (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 757, 762 (section 353, the predecessor statute to section 366.2(a), does not require commencement of an action against a personal representative within one year after the date of death).) The first amended complaint alleges that decedent passed away on December 23, 2013. Ms. Li filed this action on June 27, 2014. The filing was within the one year period of section 366.2(a). Mr. Laub's citation to Bradly v. Breen (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 798 is off point. There, the court held that a cross-complaint for indemnity that was filed 4 years after the decedent's death was untimely under section 366.2. Mr. Laub's argument that this action is time-barred because Ms. Li's creditor's claim was filed on June 13, 2016, more than two years after decedent's death, lacks merit. Ms. Li timely filed the creditor's claim approximately two months after Mr. Laub was appointed personal representative and thus Ms. Li was within the four month deadline of Probate Code 9100(a)(1). Mr. Laub contends that Ms. Li failed to comply with Probate Code 9351 because this action and the first amended complaint that named Mr. Laub were filed before the creditor's claim was filed. Such non-compliance immaterial under the facts of this case. "Property held in a revocable living trust is not subject to probate administration after the settlor dies." (Valentine v. Read (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 787, 792.) Paragraphs 5-8 of the first amended complaint allege that all of decedent's assets were held in trust, there were no assets to probate, and probate proceedings were opened merely to facilitate prosecution of this case. Plaintiff should not be disadvantaged for opening a probate proceeding in these circumstances. (Clark v. Kirby (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 1505, 1514, n.9.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
